methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Zettascale is a seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Elias Almqvist and Prithvi Raj. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector contains 14 same-sector peers, including 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers such as Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Two standard valuation methods do not apply to this analysis. Peer-median benchmarking fails due to insufficient narrow peer density: while 14 same-sector peers exist, only 2 occupy adjacent development stages, and none meet the threshold of 3+ datapoints within comparable valuation tiers. Public-comp anchoring, though the semiconductor sector median trades at 20.3x price-to-sales across the three public comparables, cannot be deployed because Zettascale's revenue is not on file—a common constraint for private companies at this stage.

The absence of applicable quantitative triangulation methods leaves this valuation suspended. To support a credible range, additional data is required: either Zettascale's trailing or projected revenue (to apply public multiples), or sufficient disclosure from seed-stage private peers in semiconductor hardware to establish a narrower comparable set. The high dispersion within the broader private peer group—spanning specialized AI accelerators, quantum computing, and analog systems—suggests that stage-adjusted transaction data or expert-derived risk factors would be necessary to proceed.
